commit.message:
fix: propagate cast errors in `add()` (#3075)

When we write data with `add()`, we can input data to the table's
schema. However, we were using "safe" mode, which propagates errors as
nulls. For example, if you pass `u64::max` into a field that is a `u32`,
it will just write null instead of giving overflow error. Now it
propagates the overflow. This is the same behavior as other systems like
DuckDB.

---------

commit.message:
Add tracking allocator for memory leak detection (#66)

* Add tracking allocator for memory leak detection (#63)

Wraps mem.Tracking_Allocator around the default context allocator in
debug builds, reporting unfreed allocations and bad frees on exit —
both to stdout and to a timestamped file in logs/.

- util/tracking_allocator.odin: TRACK_ALLOCATIONS flag (on by default
  via ODIN_DEBUG), Tracking_Allocator_State, tracking_init/allocator/
  report_and_destroy; report uses stack buffers to avoid polluting leak count
- main.odin: install tracker at top of main() via when block; defer
  fires last (LIFO) so all explicit frees complete before the report
- Makefile: debug passes -define:TRACK_ALLOCATIONS=true, release passes
  -define:TRACK_ALLOCATIONS=false (zero overhead)
- .gitignore: add logs/ so report files are not committed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add path handling for memory leak reports in tracking allocator

- Introduced `path_from_project_root` function to return file paths relative to the current working directory, enhancing privacy in leak reports.
- Updated `tracking_report_and_destroy` to utilize the new function, ensuring that file paths in reports are stripped of the project root.
- Added necessary import for string operations to support the new functionality.

This change improves the clarity and privacy of memory leak reports generated by the tracking allocator.

---------

commit.message:
Filter overlays per dimension instead of broadcasting to all maps

Overlays were applied to every BlueMap map regardless of dimension,
causing End data to appear on the Overworld map and vice versa. The
scanner also only scanned one dimension (whichever was found first).

Now scans all worlds via api.getWorlds(), produces per-world
ScanResults, and applies markers only to maps belonging to the
matching BlueMapWorld. Cache and data export are also per-world.

- Spring Boot gRPC server with 6 RPCs (Health, Stats, UploadFile, GetArtifactStatus, GetArtifactText, Find)

- Interactive wizard-style HTML documentation (16 pages with Mermaid diagrams)
- MIT license, GitHub Actions CI pipeline
- Test corpus with sample Java, Markdown, and YAML files

commit.message:
docs: update changelog for v0.1.1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(notifier): only alert operator on failures and executed actions

Redesign notification policy: agents handle everything silently,
operator only gets notified when human attention is needed.

Notify (Action Required):
- Verification failed — scaling didn't produce expected result
- Remediation failed — EOA refill or L1 failover couldn't complete
- Scaling executed — actual vCPU change applied by cost schedule

Suppressed (agent-handled):
- Cost insights — agent auto-applies schedule
- Successful remediation — agent resolved the issue
- Schedule creation without execution — no state change
- Verification success — everything is fine

commit.message:
fix: middleware matcher now skips all static files from public/

Matcher '/((?!api|_next/static|_next/image|.*\..*).*)'  excludes any
request whose path contains a file extension (.png, .js, .json, .ico,
.svg, etc.), so every static asset in public/ bypasses auth. Only
extensionless page routes go through Supabase session middleware.

Previously logo-horizontal.png, sw.js, sw-custom.js, workbox-*.js and
apple-touch-icon.png were intercepted and redirected to /login for
unauthenticated visitors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: right-click fly cam in editor mode without pointer lock

Right-click hold now activates free camera movement in editor mode
using movementX/Y directly, bypassing the pointer lock requirement
that was silently blocking all mouse look. WASD/Space/Shift move the
camera while right-click is held; releasing restores normal cursor for
object selection. Left-click-only filter added to onPointerDown so
right-click navigation doesn't accidentally deselect objects.

generic terminology (CI gates, agent runs, UI verifiers). RELOS is
an open spec anyone can adopt, not tied to any specific ecosystem.

commit.message:
fix: always delegate SWAG tasks to core VM (Ansible 2.18 compat)

The omit filter does not work with delegate_to on Ansible ≤2.18 —
the Jinja expression is passed as a literal string, causing tasks to
run on localhost (Semaphore container) where sudo is unavailable.
Always delegate to _swag_host instead.

commit.message:
[pn-bb74ce2d] Update springfield spec: double Ctrl+C for AFK loops

sgf was using single-press SIGINT semantics (AtomicBool), killing
ralph within 50ms of the first Ctrl+C before the user could press
again. Updated spec to describe sgf mirroring ralph's double-press
behavior for AFK loops while keeping single-press for non-AFK/interactive.

Created fix tasks: pn-816d6c25, pn-6b4be861, pn-138a5612, pn-856ae6aa.

trainer — just load checkpoint and run.
